9. Your shoulder blades shouldn’t pinch together at any point. Make sure to keep your spine in a neutral position and your hips parallel with the floor

name: TRX Plank
description:
1. Adjust a TRX to mid-shin height

2. With your back to the TRX, set up in a quadruped position on your hands and knees. Ensure you have a neutral spine

3. Put both into the TRX straps and make sure they’re level

4. Then go onto your forearms and grasp your hands together, leaving a 45-degree angle between your forearms

5. Begin the move by posteriorly tilting your pelvis and bracing your core. Then extend both legs back in a controlled manner, making sure to keep your core engaged

6. Your head should be in a neutral position looking at the floor and feet roughly hip-width apart

7. Create tension in your hands, forearms, and upper back to help maintain position

8. Maintain this position for the allotted time, keeping constant tension in your core and glutes

9. Your shoulder blades shouldn’t pinch together at any point. Make sure to keep your spine in a neutral position and your hips parallel with the floor

name: TRX Side Plank
description:
1. Lie on your side and put both your feet into TRX straps

2. Place your forearm against the floor and fist clenched

3. Your head should be facing forward, and you should have a neutral spine

4. Place your other hand on your top hip or flat by your side

5. Start by bracing your core and elevating your hips off the floor until your body is in a straight line

6. Maintain this position while keeping constant tension in your core and glutes

name: TRX Side Plank Knee to Chest
description:
1. Lie on your side and put both your feet into TRX straps

2. Place your forearm against the floor and fist clenched

3. Your head should be facing forward, and you should have a neutral spine

4. Place your other hand on your top hip or flat by your side

5. Start by bracing your core and elevating your hips off the floor until your body is in a straight line

6. Then bring your top leg in towards your chest. It’s important that you keep a neutral spine throughout

7. Maintain this position while keeping constant tension in your core and glutes

name: TRX Single Knee To Chest
description:
1. Adjust a TRX to mid-shin height

2. With your back to the TRX, set up in a quadruped position on your hands and knees. Ensure you have a neutral spine with your hands underneath your shoulders

3. Put both into the TRX straps and make sure they’re level

4. Posteriorly tilt your pelvis and brace your core. Then extend both legs back in a controlled manner, making sure to keep your core engaged

5. Your head should be in a neutral position looking at the floor and feet roughly hip-width apart

6. Create tension in your hands, shoulders, and upper back to help maintain position

7. Begin the move by bringing one knee in towards your chest in a slow controlled manner, making sure to keep a neutral spine in the process

8. Pause and then return to the start position in a slow controlled manner

9. Your shoulder blades shouldn’t pinch together at any point. Make sure to keep your spine in a neutral position and your hips parallel with the floor

10. Repeat on the other side

name: TRX Squat
description:
1. Start by facing a TRX and lightly grabbing a handle in both hands. Take a step back so you take off the slack and position your arms are by your side at 90-degrees

2. Foot position: Start with feet shoulder-width apart and slightly turned out. Some people will prefer a wider stance, or one with the feet turned out even more or with the toes pointed forward. Find what’s comfortable

3. Once foot position is set, screw the feet into the floor. This will help you create torque as well as grip the floor. Your bodyweight should be centred over the middle of your foot to ensure balance

4. Stand in an upright position with a neutral spine, with your knees in line with your toes

5. Start the movement by bracing your core and then bending simultaneously at both the hips and knees, slowly lowering yourself with the help of the TRX in a controlled manner to the end of your range of motion. A good marker is your thigh parallel to the ground. Everyone is different here. Find a depth that allows you to maintain correct form and a neutral spine

6. Keep your knees in line with your toes on the descent and your torso as upright as possible

7. When you reach the bottom of the squat, push through your entire foot and drive up from the bottom, ensuring constant tension in your core throughout

8. Make sure to drive your knees out on the way up and keep your chest up

9. A common fault is to use the upper body to pull yourself up rather than letting your legs do the work, so focus on pushing through your lower body

10. Pause and repeat

name: TRX Straight Arm Plank
description:
1. Adjust a TRX to mid-shin height

2. With your back to the TRX, set up in a quadruped position on your hands and knees. Ensure you have a neutral spine with your hands underneath your shoulders

3. Put both into the TRX straps and make sure they’re level

4. Begin the move by posteriorly tilting your pelvis and bracing your core. Then extend both legs back in a controlled manner, making sure to keep your core engaged

5. Your head should be in a neutral position looking at the floor and feet roughly hip-width apart

6. Create tension in your hands, shoulders, and upper back to help maintain position

7. Maintain this position for the allotted time, keeping constant tension in your core and glutes

8. Your shoulder blades shouldn’t pinch together at any point. Make sure to keep your spine in a neutral position and your hips parallel with the floor

name: Two Leg Slides
description:
1. Set up in a prone position on the floor with both legs off the floor at 90-degrees. Place your arms straight by your side or on your hips

2. Your spine should be in a neutral position with your head against the floor and your back flat

3. Begin by posteriorly tilting your pelvis and bracing your core, then drop both feet towards the floor in a slow controlled manner. Touch the floor with your heels and slide your feet as far forward as you can, while keeping your back flat to the floor and your core engaged

4. Pause slightly, then slide your feet back and return your legs to the start position in a slow controlled manner

name: Two Legs Hover To Tap
description:
1. Set up in a prone position on the floor with both legs off the floor at 90-degrees. Place your arms straight by your side or on your hips

2. Your spine should be in a neutral position with your head against the floor and your back flat

3. Begin by posteriorly tilting your pelvis and bracing your core, then drop both feet towards the floor in a slow controlled manner. Stop just short of the floor and hover your legs as far forward as possible, while keeping your back flat to the floor and your core engaged

4. Tap the floor with your heels, pause slightly, and then return your legs to the start position in a slow controlled manner

name: Unilateral Handle Press Down
description:
1. Facing a cable machine, set up a single handle at the top and grab in one hand with an overhand grip

2. Stand slightly to the side so your arm is directly in line with the cable

3. Start with your arm bent and fixed into your side. Your upper arm should be perpendicular with the floor and should stay so for the entire movement

4. Keep your shoulders pinned down and engage your core

5. Begin the movement by pressing the handle down and extending your arm until its straight, but not fully locked out

6. Keep your elbows fixed and be sure to avoid any swinging to create momentum

7. Pause at the bottom and return to the start position in a slow controlled manner, keeping constant tension in your triceps

name: Unilateral Rope Pressdown
description:
1. Facing a cable machine, set up a rope at the top and grab with one hand

2. Hand should be in a neutral position, palm facing in

3. Stand slightly to the side so your arm is directly in line with the cable

4. Start with your arm bent and fixed into your side. Your upper arm should be perpendicular with the floor and should stay so for the entire movement

5. Keep your shoulders pinned down and engage your core

6. Begin the movement by pressing the rope down and extending your arm until its straight, but not fully locked out. Your hand should finish slightly behind your body

7. Keep your elbows fixed and be sure to avoid any swinging to create momentum

8. Pause at the bottom and return to the start position in a slow controlled manner, keeping constant tension in your triceps

name: Upper Body Warm Up (Pull Focused)
description:
(Foam Rolling)

Foam Roller - Thoracic Spine: 30 sec
Foam Roller - Lats: 30 sec

(Mobility)

Cat-Cow x 5
Half-Kneeling Rotation with Forward Lean: 10 per side
3 Part Shoulder Warm-Up: 10 of each

(Activation)

Band Straight Arm Pulldown: 1 set x 10 (Hold for 2-3 secs at bottom)

name: Upper Body Warm Up (Push Focused)
description:
(Foam Rolling)

Foam Roller - Thoracic Spine: 30 sec
Foam Roller - Lats: 30 sec
Foam Roller - Chest: 30 sec

(Mobility)

Kneeling Active Lat Stretch: 10
Foam Roller Thread The Needle: 10 per side
Foam Roller - Angels: 10
3 Part Shoulder Warm-Up: 10 of each

(Activation)

Lock Big 3: 1 set x 20-25 of each movement

name: Upper Trap Stretch
description:
1. Can do stood or seated

2. Ensure you have a neutral spine and you have a nice upright posture

3. Start by turning your head to the side and placing your hand on the back/side of your head.

4. Then carefully pull your head down so you feel the stretch. At the same time push your other hand towards the floor.

5. You should feel a stretch in your trap, between your shoulder and neck area

6. Be careful not to pull down too hard and make sure you maintain posture throughout

7. Hold for allotted time

name: Walking Lunges
description:
1. Start with feet shoulder-width apart and facing forward

2. Begin by engaging your core and taking a step forward with one leg. While moving the leg in a controlled manner, bend at the knee until you reach a 90-degree angle

3. Keep on the toes with the back foot

4. The back leg should also be in a 90-degree angle and should be kept stationary throughout this movement

5. Be careful not to hit the back knee off the floor with any force and that the forward knee doesn’t track too far over your toes

6. Make sure to keep a neutral spine with an upright posture, your eyes focused out in front

7. From here, step forward by driving through the floor with the front leg and stepping in with the trail leg so you finish in a standing position

name: Walkout
description:
1. Start with feet shoulder-width apart and facing forward

2. Engage your core and attain a neutral spine

3. Start by performing a toe touch allowing the knees to bend so you can reach the floor with the hands flat

4. Then walk your hands out to a stable plank position, keeping a neutral spine and your core engaged, making sure not to hyperextend your back

4. Pause in this position and then hinge at the hips and walk your hands back towards your feet, returning to the start position

name: Weighted Seated Heel Raise
description: Weight should be placed on your knee so it's directly over your shin.

name: Wide Grip Pull Up.
description:
1. Grasp a pull-up bar with an overhand grip and place them wider than shoulder-width. Stand on a step, bench, etc if needed

2. Engage your core and ensure you have a neutral spine

3. If elevated up, begin by carefully stepping forward off the step until you’re hanging with your arms straight and holding your full bodyweight

4. Either place your legs straight down and feet slightly in front or hook them behind you. Keep a neutral spine for the entire movement

5. Then depress your shoulder blades down and pull yourself up towards the bar, driving your elbows down in the process

6. Pull up until your chin clears the bar, keeping your eyes and head level for the entire move

7. Pause at the top and then return to the start position in a slow controlled manner, ensuring constant tension in your lats, core, and upper back
